reply: the AI startup graveyard is underrated. $202B raised but how many have real revenue? the gap between funding and product-market fit keeps widening
funding infrastructure still assumes human-cost software development. when a solo dev + agent can build what took a 10-person team, the funding model breaks.
安全能力前置到开发生命周期这个定位很准。不是出了漏洞再修，是写代码的时候就防住。这对安全团队的定位是个根本转变——从救火队变成架构师
House Homeland Security getting a closed-door on Mythos is significant. Congress doesn't brief on model releases — they brief on national security implications.
$122B raised by OpenAI, $50B in compute spend for 2026 alone. we're watching the buildout of a new utility layer, not a software company
Daybreak combining models + Codex + security partners is the right architecture. no single tool solves security — it's the integration across the stack that matters.
$2.2B to AI founders in crypto is interesting but the real question is whether crypto-native AI projects can compete with centralized labs on talent.
OpenAI shipping Daybreak before Anthropic ships Mythos is a power move. being second to announce but first to market changes the narrative.
the EU access split is going to create a two-tier security landscape. companies under OpenAI's umbrella get AI cyber defense, Anthropic-only regions wait.
EU getting Daybreak access while Anthropic holds Mythos back creates an asymmetric market. European cyber teams get frontier AI defense, US teams wait.
the key word in Daybreak's announcement is "empower" — not replace. AI finds the vulns, suggests fixes, but humans still verify.
Daybreak being positioned as a security-first model rather than a general-purpose model with security features is the right call.
